body.u: Looking back over my past, I suppose I can be fairly satisfied. After leaving school I got the apprentishipapprenticeship I wanted, and in the five years it took for me to learn my trade, I did have some fun while I was still in my youthful stages. I had good mates and we knew how to enjoy ourselves. After all, it’s no good working and slaving your heart out it you can’t have leisure and freedom to do what you want.

Later on in my life, I can’t say getting married was a bad thing. I know I perhaps wouldn’t have the freedom i once had but we got on allright, and it was worthwhile. As for work, well as soon as I finished my trade, I left, and got a job as a {mate?} in Sheerness Docks. I found it was no easy work and the money a lot better than doing my trade work. I also took the occasion day off, and then I would do my favourite pastime, fishing. I also went out in my dinghy I had ever since I was sixteen , and especially if it was a calm day, I’d chug out theirthere in midwater, and just sit theirthere, and take it all in. This was the perfect thing, and now I still admit I love having a {dab?} in the sea. As time went by, I packed in my job, and I got a job as a mate on a fishing boat. This was I think a great move as I was now doing the thing I loved. We had some great times in that boat, and later on, I was forced to leave had the boat got smashed in a storm. Up to then I was fairly lucky job wise but now I had to go back for my profession, and there I remarried until I retired. I had become very slow, and the whole face of the world had seemed to have changed since I was a youngster.
